researchSources: public: github.com homepage/footer navigation visible on 2026-07-04
public: GitHub category knowledge as a code hosting and collaboration platform
assumption: sample cohort covers unauthenticated visitors because no real accounts are used

audienceSegments.name: Open-source contributor
audienceSegments.evidence: Public platform vocabulary emphasizes repositories, docs, community, GitHub CLI/Desktop/Mobile, and developer ecosystem.

audienceSegments.name: Engineering team evaluator
audienceSegments.evidence: Public navigation includes Enterprise, Security, Copilot, Pricing, Team, and Professional Services.

audienceSegments.name: Learning developer
audienceSegments.evidence: Public navigation includes Education, Skills, Docs, Community Forum, and developer newsletter.

audienceSegments.name: Mobile checker
audienceSegments.evidence: GitHub Mobile is a public product path; mobile visitors may inspect links and trust cues.
jobs: check a repo or docs quickly
see if mobile browsing is usable

masterPlan.reason: Mira's skepticism and Sol's team-risk lens should sharpen each other's judgment quickly.
Niko needs a clearer path, and Tara's impatience will press the site to prove itself fast.
Ren's hope and Ari's caution make a useful test of whether credibility feels earned.
Vale's doubt and June's playful CTO lens should expose both beginner friction and executive proof gaps.
Rook and Iris should almost align, so their separate judgments reveal whether the product stands alone.
Mira's beginner skepticism and Sol's CTO doubt create pressure around clarity and proof.
Niko's curiosity and Tara's impatience should turn vague credibility signals into a sharper verdict.
Ren's playful beginner energy and Ari's CTO curiosity should test whether the site welcomes both learning and evaluation.
Vale's impatience and June's hope should reveal whether contribution signals can overcome managerial risk.
Rook and Iris should miss each other so their independent paths show where timing alone changes confidence.

errorBots.endReason: hit a bug or dead end
errors: follow destiny: thoughtful-betabot-006 follow destiny timed out after 60000ms
click next navigation: thoughtful-betabot-006 click next navigation timed out after 15000ms
ideas: show a clear first screen for team security, reliability, and collaboration proof instead of leading with broad positioning.
Give engineering managers a fast path to team controls, compliance, uptime history, and rollout planning from the first screen.

avatar.seed: 20260630|GitHub|thoughtful-betabot-006|Ari 1|engineering manager evaluating team collaboration and security|My team already uses several tools, and every platform decision creates migration and compliance risk.|A colleague said GitHub might consolidate code review, security, and AI assistance.|Decide whether GitHub feels credible enough for a team evaluation.|Protect my team's delivery reputation by choosing stable engineering systems.|guarded|high

errorBots.lifeGoal: Protect my team's delivery reputation by choosing stable engineering systems.

mortality.lifeGoal: Protect my team's delivery reputation by choosing stable engineering systems.

topIdeas: show a fast contributor credibility path from the front page, like active projects, maintainer signals, and first-PR routes.
show security, collaboration, and enterprise stability proof immediately for leaders making platform decisions.
show a clear beginner route like Learn Git, Make First Project, Build Portfolio before the bigger product choices.
put concrete trust signals and outcome proof above the fold for technical buyers.
show contribution health signals early, such as recent maintainer activity, open beginner issues, and response patterns.
show a clear first screen for team security, reliability, and collaboration proof instead of leading with broad positioning.
Give engineering managers a fast path to team controls, compliance, uptime history, and rollout planning from the first screen.
show a clear "Start learning GitHub" path for beginners before the product menu gets dense.
Give startup teams a fast evidence path with customer scale, security posture, pricing clarity, and Copilot impact in one compact proof panel.
Give contributors a fast credibility lens for any repository: activity, maintainer responsiveness, good first issues, and contribution guide quality.
show a clear path from the homepage to enterprise security, migration, and team governance proof.
Add a clear beginner path like “New to GitHub?” with three calm steps: create account, learn repositories, make first contribution.
Add a beginner path that starts with one project goal and explains which feature to use first, second, and later.
Put security, reliability, and small-team outcomes directly beside the main sign-up path.
Give small technical teams a direct proof path with concrete outcomes, security posture, and setup effort upfront.
Give newcomers a fast path to assess a project’s activity, maintainer responsiveness, and first-contribution options.
Give contributors a fast credibility view with maintainer activity, issue quality, and first-contribution signals.
Give engineering leaders a direct path to reliability, governance, and migration confidence from the first screen.
Give engineering managers a direct evaluation path that compares collaboration, security, governance, and migration effort in one view.
Add a clear beginner route near the top, like "Start learning GitHub" with three plain steps.
Add a beginner route that says, “Start here if you know Git basics,” with three clear next steps.
Give small technical teams a fast proof path with security posture, admin controls, AI limits, pricing, and customer results in one comparison view.
Put concrete security outcomes, adoption examples, and small-team decision paths directly near the enterprise pitch.
Give contributors a direct path to discover active repositories with maintainer responsiveness, beginner issues, and recent activity surfaced upfront.
Give contributors a quick credibility panel for activity, maintainer response, open beginner issues, and contribution docs.
Give managers a direct path to collaboration controls, governance, uptime, and security evidence from the first screen.
Give engineering managers a compact evaluation path that compares collaboration, security, and administration needs for small teams versus enterprise teams.
Give team leads a short evaluation path that connects security, migrations, permissions, and collaboration setup in one checklist.
Give engineering leaders a compact comparison focused on control, audit needs, support, and rollout risk.
Add a clear beginner route like “Start learning GitHub” beside sign-up and Copilot prompts.
Add a beginner mode that turns the Features page into a short path based on my goal and comfort level.
Put a CTO path up front with proof points for small teams: reliability, permissions, AI governance, and migration effort.
Give startup teams a proof-first path that compares speed, security, and setup effort in one clear view.
